sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The board has transformed again! The layout is completely different now. Looking at the image:
- The green blocks are now at different positions
- There's a green block at the top row (3 green blocks)
- There are 2 green blocks in the middle (vertical)
- There are 3 green blocks at the bottom right
- The purple blocks are at the bottom right (3 of them) and one at the top right
- The player is on the left side
I need to check the current green block positions and figure out the new strategy. Let me inspect the segmentation to find all green blocks.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Board transformed again! Check current green blocks
seg = current_frame.segmentation
greens = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N' and n['pixels'] > 5]
print(f"Green blocks: {len(greens)}")
for g in greens:
    b = g['boundary']
    center = (sum(r[0] for r in b)//len(b), sum(r[1] for r in b)//len(b))
    print(f"  center={center}")

purple = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p']
print(f"Purple objects: {len(purple)}")
for p in purple:
    b = p['boundary']
    center = (sum(r[0] for r in b)//len(b), sum(r[1] for r in b)//len(b))
    print(f"  pixels={p['pixels']} center={center}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Looking at the green blocks:
- (9, 15), (9, 21), (9, 27) - top row
